steps.assistant_text: One-shot FP state-advance trigger (54 insns, 0xD8). Structural decode was correct; the 12-word near-miss was two C-steerable as1/ugen shape issues, NOT a true FP-scheduling cap. Lever 1 (store-order): the target emits the two integer stores 0x7C4=1 / 0x7C8-=1 BEFORE the FP stores 0x7A8/0x7AC; moving those two int statements ahead of the x+4/y-4 stores in source dropped 12->6 diffs. Lever 2 (const-first FP-reg coloring): the 1.0f written to 0x7C0 must color $f10 (low) and the add/sub results $f16/$f18 (high); IDO numbers FP pseudos in source-statement order, so placing the `*(0x7C0)=one;` assignment BEFORE the x+4/y-4 stores creates the const pseudo first -> $f10, pushing the arith results up to $f16/$f18 (target). 6->0. Kept `float two=2.0f; /two` (forces div.s-by-literal + target-like bnel returns). Tail callee resolved from the .s reloc: gl_func_00000000 placeholder -> real mgrproc_uso_func_0002DC(50) (both jal-encode 0C000000 under USO R_MIPS_26 runtime reloc, ROM unchanged).

metadata.verification: reloc-filtered word-compare vs expected/.o = 0 non-reloc diffs + reloc-set match (R_MIPS_26 -> mgrproc_uso_func_0002DC); matching build/.o byte-exact; full make exit 0 + ROM cmp byte-identical to baserom.z64
